prompt: Using the provided reference timetable sheet, transform it into a bold contemporary graphic travel poster / magazine-spread design while preserving the overall school-trip schedule structure and key itinerary information. Replace the plain document layout with a dynamic diagonal composition, oversized brush-calligraphy Japanese headline 旅のしおり, and smaller title text for a 2泊3日 trip toward 関西方面. Organize the schedule into exactly 3 large vertical day sections: Day 01 Kyoto, Day 02 Nara / Osaka, and Day 03 Osaka, keeping the times and main activities from the reference but typesetting them as clean poster text with a thin timeline line. Add high-contrast neon accent graphics in hot pink, cyan, yellow, blue, green, and purple, with angular triangles, paint splashes, speed lines, halftone textures, arrows, scribbles, and collage-like overlays. Add photographic/illustrated travel imagery not present in the reference: temples and shrine architecture for Kyoto, a deer for Nara, Osaka Castle, a busy Osaka street scene, and Tsutenkaku/tower imagery, integrated as tilted monochrome or duotone cutout panels. Include small English slogan text such as "SCHOOL TRIP 2024", "MOVE. SEE. FEEL. JOURNEY.", "NO PLAN NO LIFE JUST GO.", and day-theme labels like HISTORY, PAST & PRESENT, and FREEDOM. Keep a mostly off-white paper background, black typography, energetic editorial street-design styling, and make the final result look like a polished youth travel booklet cover/schedule poster rather than a formal handout.
